The heavyweight boxer is famous for his incredible power punches.
He is also well known for his very aggressive fighting style.
This fighter has faced a professional opponent in several matches.
Their first match was a highly anticipated event for fans.
In that initial bout, he successfully landed exactly 120 power punches.
The crowd was amazed by the sheer force of his strikes.
The second match between these two fighters was equally exciting.
During this second encounter, he increased his punch output significantly.
He managed to land 25% more power punches than in the first fight.
To find the number for the second match, we must calculate the increase.
Twenty-five percent of 120 is equal to thirty additional punches.
Adding those thirty punches to the original 120 gives us the new total.
Therefore, he landed 150 power punches in their second match.
The momentum continued as the two boxers met for a third time.
In this third match, his performance was even more impressive than before.
He landed thirty more power punches than he did in the second match.
Since he threw 150 in the second fight, we add thirty to that number.
This calculation shows he landed 180 power punches in the third match.
Now we need to find the total number of punches across all three fights.
We start by adding the 120 punches from the very first match.
Next, we add the 150 punches from the second match to our sum.
Then we add the 180 punches from the third match to the running total.
The sum of 120 and 150 is equal to 270 punches so far.
Adding the 180 from the final match brings the total to 450.
So the boxer landed a grand total of 450 power punches in all.
This impressive statistic highlights his dominance in the ring.
His aggressive style clearly paid off during these three specific matches.
Fans will remember these numbers for a very long time to come.
The progression from 120 to 150 to 180 shows his growing intensity.
It is rare to see such a consistent increase in power output.
This series of matches will likely be studied by boxing analysts.
The total of 450 punches represents a monumental achievement in boxing history.
